note: Views Fieldsets adds one Views field handler (id `fieldset`) plus theme hooks and an alter hook. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, how do I group several fields of a View inside a fieldset/details box? Name the Views field I add.
evals.expected_output: Add the Views field with handler id `fieldset` (labeled "Global: Fieldset"), then in the Rearrange screen drag other fields underneath it to make them children.

evals.prompt: What wrapper types can the Views Fieldsets field render into, and which option controls it?
evals.expected_output: The `wrapper` option chooses one of `details`, `fieldset`, or `div` (default `fieldset`).

evals.prompt: Which settings does the Fieldset Views field expose, and can the legend use tokens?
evals.expected_output: Options: fields (children), wrapper, legend, classes, collapsible, collapsed. The legend and classes support Views row tokens like {{ title }}.

evals.prompt: In Views Fieldsets, what happens to a group whose child fields are all empty?
evals.expected_output: The wrapper is only output when at least one child has non-empty content (show_fieldset); otherwise the fieldset is not rendered.

evals.prompt: I want to add a custom wrapper type (e.g. a Bootstrap card) to the Views Fieldsets wrapper dropdown. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_views_fieldsets_wrapper_types_alter(array &$data) to add a key => label entry, and provide a matching views_fieldsets_<key> theme hook/template.

evals.prompt: Which theme hooks / Twig templates does Views Fieldsets provide for overriding a group's markup?
evals.expected_output: Three: views_fieldsets_fieldset, views_fieldsets_details, views_fieldsets_div (templates views-fieldsets-fieldset/details/div.html.twig), with per-view/display/field theme suggestions.

evals.prompt: What are Views Fieldsets' module dependencies and does it add a config UI, permissions, or Drush commands?
evals.expected_output: It needs only Views (core); no separate config UI route, no permissions, no Drush commands. Configuration lives inside each view.
